chronos.piPath.description: Path to the pi binary. Leave empty to use "pi" from PATH.

chronos.autoStartSession.description: Automatically open the Chronos panel and start an agent session when a Chronos workspace (a folder containing .chronos/) is opened. Only auto-starts when pi and the Chronos pi-package are already installed; otherwise start it manually with "Chronos: Start Agent Session".

chronos.piPackageSource.markdownDescription: **Dev override.** Source the setup flow installs the Chronos pi-package from. Accepts a local path (`/abs/path/to/chronos/chronos`), a git URL, or a git URL with a branch/tag using the `@` form (`https://github.com/ai-historian/chronos@my-branch`). Leave empty to use the GitHub release pinned to this extension's version.

chronos.piNpmPackage.markdownDescription: **Dev override.** npm package the setup flow installs for the `pi` CLI when pi isn't found. Leave empty for the default `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent` (the maintained package). Set to override it — e.g. a fork or a version-pinned tag like `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent@0.79.10`.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with Chronos
walkthroughs.description: Install the agent, scaffold a workspace, connect an AI provider, and start digitizing historical sources.

steps.title: Install dependencies
steps.description:
Chronos runs on the `pi` agent CLI plus the Chronos pi-package — installed once per machine.
[Install dependencies](command:chronos.setup)

steps.title: Initialize a workspace
steps.description:
Open a folder, then scaffold it into a Chronos workspace (sources/, data/, memory/, …).
[Init workspace](command:chronos.init)

steps.title: Start an agent session
steps.description:
Open the Chronos panel — page viewer plus chat — to begin working.
[Start session](command:chronos.startSession)

steps.title: Connect an AI provider
steps.description:
Store an API key (Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, …) the agent will use. Use the **Log in** button in the panel header, or run the command.
[Connect provider](command:chronos.login)
